expression.description: The expression value in your chosen language syntax.

trimResult.description: Whether to trim the returned values when this language is in use.

pretty.description: To pretty format the output (only JSon or XML supported).

nested.label: advanced
nested.description: If the result is a nested simple expression should this expression be evaluated as well.

resultType.description: The class of the result type (type from output).

trim.label: advanced
trim.description: Whether to trim the source code to remove leading and trailing whitespaces and line breaks.

abs(exp).label: number
abs(exp).description: Converts the message body (or expression) to a long number and return the absolute value.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

assert(exp,msg).label: condition
assert(exp,msg).description: Evaluates the expression and throws an exception with the message if the condition is false

params.name: exp
params.description: The predicate expression to evaluate

params.name: msg
params.description: The error message if the assertion fails

a2a:emit(message).label: a2a,ai
a2a:emit(message).description: Emits an A2A task progress update with WORKING state. Requires the camel-a2a component on the classpath and an active A2A task context.

params.name: message
params.description: The progress message. The message may contain nested Simple expressions

a2a:emit(state,message).label: a2a,ai
a2a:emit(state,message).description: Emits an A2A task progress update with the given task state. Requires the camel-a2a component on the classpath and an active A2A task context.

params.name: state
params.description: The A2A task state, such as WORKING, INPUT_REQUIRED, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELED, REJECTED, or AUTH_REQUIRED

params.name: message
params.description: The progress message. The message may contain nested Simple expressions

a2a:text.label: a2a,ai
a2a:text.description: Extracts TextPart content from an A2A Task or Message in the message body.

a2a:text(expression).label: a2a,ai
a2a:text(expression).description: Extracts TextPart content from an A2A Task or Message returned by the expression.

params.name: expression
params.description: The expression that returns an A2A Task or Message

a2a:data.label: a2a,ai
a2a:data.description: Extracts DataPart content as a JSON string from an A2A Task or Message in the message body.

a2a:data(expression).label: a2a,ai
a2a:data(expression).description: Extracts DataPart content as a JSON string from an A2A Task or Message returned by the expression.

params.name: expression
params.description: The expression that returns an A2A Task or Message

a2a:file.label: a2a,ai
a2a:file.description: Extracts FilePart content from an A2A Task or Message in the message body.

a2a:file(expression).label: a2a,ai
a2a:file(expression).description: Extracts FilePart content from an A2A Task or Message returned by the expression.

params.name: expression
params.description: The expression that returns an A2A Task or Message

a2a:card.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card as a JSON string.

a2a:card.name.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.name.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card name.

a2a:card.description.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.description.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card description.

a2a:card.url.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.url.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card URL.

a2a:card.version.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.version.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card version.

a2a:card.skills.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.skills.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card skills as human-readable text.

a2a:card.skills.json.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.skills.json.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card skills as a JSON array string.

a2a:card.iconUrl.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.iconUrl.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card icon URL.

a2a:card.documentationUrl.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.documentationUrl.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card documentation URL.

a2a:card.provider.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.provider.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card provider as a JSON string.

a2a:card.capabilities.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.capabilities.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card capabilities as a JSON string.

a2a:card.supportedInterfaces.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.supportedInterfaces.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card supported interfaces as a JSON array string.

a2a:card.securitySchemes.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.securitySchemes.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card security schemes as a JSON object string.

a2a:card.securityRequirements.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.securityRequirements.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card security requirements as a JSON array string.

a2a:card.security.label: a2a,ai
a2a:card.security.description: Returns the resolved A2A agent card legacy security field as a JSON array string.

attachment.name.label: attachment
attachment.name.description: The DataHandler for the given attachment.

attachments.label: attachment
attachments.description: All the attachments as a Map<String,DataHandler.

attachmentsContentAs(name,type).label: attachment
attachmentsContentAs(name,type).description: The content of the attachment, converted to the given type.

attachmentsContentAsText.name.label: attachment
attachmentsContentAsText.name.description: The content of the attachment as text (ie String).

attachmentsHeader(key,name).label: attachment
attachmentsHeader(key,name).description: The attachment header with the given name.

attachmentsHeaderAs(key,name,type).label: attachment
attachmentsHeaderAs(key,name,type).description: The attachment header with the given name, converted to the given type.

attachmentsSize.label: attachment
attachmentsSize.description: The number of attachments. Is 0 if there are no attachments.

average(val...).label: number
average(val...).description: Returns the average number from all the values (integral numbers only).

params.name: val
params.description: Values to average. When omitted uses the message body (list or array)

base64Decode(exp).label: base64
base64Decode(exp).description: Base64 decodes the message body (or expression)

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

base64Encode(exp).label: base64
base64Encode(exp).description: Base64 encodes the message body (or expression)

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

bean(name.method).label: core
bean(name.method).description: Calls a Java bean. The name of the bean can also refer to a class name using type prefix as follows `bean:type:com.foo.MyClass`. If no method name is given then Camel will automatic attempt to find the best method to use.

params.name: name
params.description: The bean name (or type:classname)

params.name: method
params.description: The method name to invoke

examples: ${bean(type:com.foo.MyClass.myMethod)} -> result // calls static method by classname

body.label: core
examples: ${body} -> Hello World // when body is the string 'Hello World'

bodyAs(type).label: core
bodyAs(type).description: Converts the message body to the given type (classname).

params.name: type
params.description: The target type classname (e.g. String, int, byte[])

bodyOneLine.label: function
bodyOneLine.description: Converts the body to a String and removes all line-breaks, so the string is in one line.

bodyType.label: core
examples: ${bodyType} -> java.lang.String // when body is a String

capitalize(exp).label: string
capitalize(exp).description: Capitalizes the message body/expression as a String value (upper case every words)

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

ceil(exp).label: number
ceil(exp).description: Converts the message body (or expression) to a floating number and return the ceil value (rounded up to nearest integer).

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

clearAttachments.label: attachment
clearAttachments.description: Removes all existing attachments on the message.

collate(num).displayName: Group Message Body into Sub Lists
collate(num).label: collection
collate(num).description: The collate function iterates the message body and groups the data into sub lists of specified size. This can be used with the Splitter EIP to split a message body and group/batch the split sub message into a group of N sub lists.

params.name: num
params.description: The group size for each sub list

concat(exp,exp,separator).label: string
concat(exp,exp,separator).description: Performs a string concat using two expressions (message body as default) with optional separator

params.name: exp
params.description: First expression

params.name: exp
params.description: Second expression

params.name: separator
params.description: The separator between the two expressions

convertTo(exp,type).label: core
convertTo(exp,type).description: Converts the message body (or expression) to the specified type.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression to convert

params.name: type
params.description: The target type classname

contains(exp,text).label: condition
contains(exp,text).description: Returns true if the message body (or expression) contains part of the text (ignore case)

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression to check

params.name: text
params.description: The text to search for

date(command).label: date
date(command).description: Evaluates to a java.util.Date object. Supported commands are: `now` for current timestamp, `millis` for current timestamp in millis (unix epoch), `exchangeCreated` for the timestamp when the current exchange was created, `header.xxx` to use the Long/Date object in the header with the key xxx. `variable.xxx` to use the Long/Date in the variable with the key xxx. `exchangeProperty.xxx` to use the Long/Date object in the exchange property with the key xxx. `file` for the last modified timestamp of the file (available with a File consumer). Command accepts offsets such as: `now-24h` or `header.xxx+1h` or even `now+1h30m-100`.

params.name: command
params.description: The date command (now, millis, exchangeCreated, header.xxx, variable.xxx, file). Supports offsets like now-24h

date-with-timezone(command:timezone:patt.label: date
date-with-timezone(command:timezone:patt.description: Formats the date to a String using the given date pattern, and with support for timezone. Supported commands are: `now` for current timestamp, `exchangeCreated` for the timestamp when the current exchange was created, `header.xxx` to use the Long/Date object in the header with the key xxx. `variable.xxx` to use the Long/Date in the variable with the key xxx. `exchangeProperty.xxx` to use the Long/Date object in the exchange property with the key xxx. `file` for the last modified timestamp of the file (available with a File consumer). Command accepts offsets such as: `now-24h` or `header.xxx+1h` or even `now+1h30m-100`.

params.name: command
params.description: The date command (now, exchangeCreated, header.xxx, variable.xxx, file). Supports offsets

params.name: timezone
params.description: The timezone (e.g. UTC, Europe/Paris, America/New_York)

params.name: pattern
params.description: The date pattern using java.text.SimpleDateFormat syntax

distinct(val...).label: collection
distinct(val...).description: Returns a set of all the values with duplicates removed

params.name: val
params.description: Values to de-duplicate. When omitted uses the message body (list or array)

empty(type).label: collection
empty(type).description: Creates a new empty object (decided by type). Use `string` to create an empty String. Use `list` to create an empty `java.util.ArrayList`. Use `map` to create an empty `java.util.LinkedHashMap`. Use `set` to create an empty `java.util.LinkedHashSet`.

env.name.label: other
env.name.description: The OS environment variable with the given name

params.name: name
params.description: The environment variable name

exchangeProperty.name.label: core
exchangeProperty.name.description: The exchange property with the given name

params.name: name
params.description: The exchange property name

exception.label: core
exception.description: The exception object on the exchange (also from caught exceptions), is null if no exception present.

exception.message.label: core
exception.message.description: The exception message (also from caught exceptions), is null if no exception present.

exception.stackTrace.label: core
exception.stackTrace.description: The exception stacktrace (also from caught exceptions), is null if no exception present.

filter(exp,fun).label: collection
filter(exp,fun).description: Returns a List containing the values that satisfy the predicate function (returning true)

params.name: exp
params.description: The input collection expression

params.name: fun
params.description: The predicate function that returns true/false

forEach(exp,fun).label: collection
forEach(exp,fun).description: Returns a List containing the values returned by the function when applied to each value from the input expression

params.name: exp
params.description: The input collection expression

params.name: fun
params.description: The function to apply to each element

floor(exp).label: number
floor(exp).description: Converts the message body (or expression) to a floating number and return the floor value (rounded down to nearest integer).

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

fromRouteId.label: core
fromRouteId.description: The original route id where this exchange was created.

function(name,exp).label: core
function(name,exp).description: Invokes a custom function with the given name using the message body (or expression) as input parameter.

params.name: name
params.description: The function name (registered in the Camel context)

params.name: exp
params.description: The input expression. When omitted uses the message body

hash(exp,algorithm).label: other
hash(exp,algorithm).description: Returns a hashed value (string in hex decimal) of the message body/expression using JDK MessageDigest. The algorithm can be SHA-256 (default) or SHA3-256.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression to hash. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: algorithm
params.description: The hash algorithm (SHA-256 or SHA3-256)

header.name.label: core
header.name.description: The message header with the given name

params.name: name
params.description: The header name

headerAs(key,type).label: core
headerAs(key,type).description: Converts the message header to the given type (classname).

params.name: key
params.description: The header name

params.name: type
params.description: The target type classname

headers.label: core
headers.description: Returns all the message headers in a Map

hostName.label: other
hostName.description: Returns the local hostname (may be empty if not possible to resolve).

htmlClean(exp).label: html
htmlClean(exp).description: Cleans the HTML to remove unsafe links and JavaScripts from the message body (or expression)

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression containing HTML. When omitted uses the message body

htmlDecode(exp).label: html
htmlDecode(exp).description: Decodes the HTML as plain text (removing all HTML tags) that is also suitable as input for AI and LLMs

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression containing HTML. When omitted uses the message body

htmlParse(exp).label: html
htmlParse(exp).description: Parses the HTML as a JSoup object from the message body (or expression)

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression containing HTML. When omitted uses the message body

iif(predicate,trueExp,falseExp).label: condition
iif(predicate,trueExp,falseExp).description: Evaluates the predicate and returns the value of trueExp or falseExp. This function is similar to the ternary operator in Java.

params.name: predicate
params.description: The predicate expression to evaluate

params.name: trueExp
params.description: The expression to return when predicate is true

params.name: falseExp
params.description: The expression to return when predicate is false

examples: ${iif(${header.admin} == true,'Admin','User')} -> Admin // when header admin is true

isAlpha(exp).label: condition
isAlpha(exp).description: Whether the message body (or expression) is alphabetic value (A..Z). For more advanced checks use the `regex` operator.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

isAlphaNumeric(exp).label: condition
isAlphaNumeric(exp).description: Whether the message body (or expression) is alphanumeric value (A..Z0-9). For more advanced checks use the `regex` operator.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

isEmpty(exp).label: condition
isEmpty(exp).description: Whether the message body (or expression) is null or empty (list/map types are tested if they have 0 elements).

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

examples: ${isEmpty(${body})} -> true // when body is null or empty string
${isEmpty(${header.name})} -> false // when header has a value

isNumeric(exp).label: condition
isNumeric(exp).description: Whether the message body (or expression) is numeric value (0..9). For more advanced checks use the `regex` operator.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

jq(input,exp).label: json
jq(input,exp).description: When working with JSon data, then this allows using the JQ language, for example, to extract data from the message body (in JSon format). This requires having camel-jq JAR on the classpath. For input (optional), you can choose `header:key`, `exchangeProperty:key` or `variable:key` to use as input for the JSon payload instead of the message body.

params.name: input
params.description: The input source (header:key, exchangeProperty:key, variable:key). When omitted uses the message body

params.name: exp
params.description: The JQ expression

examples: ${jq('.name')} -> John // extracts name field from JSON body
${jq(header:myHeader,'.items[0]')} -> first item from JSON in header

jsonpath(input,exp).label: json
jsonpath(input,exp).description: When working with JSon data, then this allows using the JSonPath language, for example, to extract data from the message body (in JSon format). This requires having camel-jsonpath JAR on the classpath. For input (optional), you can choose `header:key`, `exchangeProperty:key` or `variable:key` to use as input for the JSon payload instead of the message body.

params.name: input
params.description: The input source (header:key, exchangeProperty:key, variable:key). When omitted uses the message body

params.name: exp
params.description: The JSonPath expression

examples: ${jsonpath('$.name')} -> John // extracts name from JSON body
${jsonpath(header:myHeader,'$.items[0].id')} -> first item id from JSON in header

join(separator,prefix,exp).label: string
join(separator,prefix,exp).description: The join function iterates the message body/expression and joins the data into a string. The separator is by default a comma. The prefix is optional. The join uses the message body as source by default. It is possible to refer to another source (simple language) such as a header via the exp parameter. For example `join('&','id=','$\{header.ids}')`.

params.name: separator
params.description: The separator between values

params.name: prefix
params.description: The prefix for each value

params.name: exp
params.description: The input collection. When omitted uses the message body

length(exp).label: core
length(exp).description: The payload length (number of bytes) of the message body (or expression).

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

list(val...).label: collection
list(val...).description: The list function creates an ArrayList with the given set of values.

params.name: val
params.description: The values to include in the list

listAdd(source,exp).label: collection
listAdd(source,exp).description: Adds the result of the expression to the message body (or expression) which is a list

params.name: source
params.description: The source list. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: exp
params.description: The value to add to the list

listRemove(source,exp).label: collection
listRemove(source,exp).description: Removes the result of the expression from the message body (or expression) which is a list

params.name: source
params.description: The source list. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: exp
params.description: The value to remove from the list

load(file).label: core
load(file).description: Loads the content of the resource from classpath (cannot load from file-system to avoid dangerous situations).

params.name: file
params.description: The classpath resource to load (e.g. classpath:myTemplate.txt)

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

mandatoryBodyAs(type).label: core
mandatoryBodyAs(type).description: Converts the message body to the given type (classname). If the body is null then the function will fail with an exception

params.name: type
params.description: The target type classname (e.g. String, int, byte[])

examples: ${mandatoryBodyAs(String)} -> body as string // throws if body is null

map(key1,value1,...).label: collection
map(key1,value1,...).description: The map function creates a LinkedHashMap with the given set of pairs.

params.name: key1
params.description: The first key

params.name: value1
params.description: The first value

mapAdd(source,key,exp).label: collection
mapAdd(source,key,exp).description: Adds the result of the expression to the message body (or expression) which is a map

params.name: source
params.description: The source map. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: key
params.description: The key to add

params.name: exp
params.description: The value expression to add

mapRemove(source,key).label: collection
mapRemove(source,key).description: Removes the result of the expression from the message body (or expression) which is a map

params.name: source
params.description: The source map. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: key
params.description: The key to remove

max(val...).label: number
max(val...).description: Returns the maximum number from all the values (integral numbers only).

params.name: val
params.description: Values to compare. When omitted uses the message body (list or array)

messageAs(type).label: core
messageAs(type).description: Converts the message to the given type (classname).

params.name: type
params.description: The target type classname

messageHistory(boolean).label: core
messageHistory(boolean).description: The message history of the current exchange (how it has been routed). This is similar to the route stack-trace message history the error handler logs in case of an unhandled exception. The boolean can be used to turn off detailed information to be less verbose, and avoid printing sensitive data from the message.

params.name: boolean
params.description: Whether to include detailed information (headers, body)

messageTimestamp.label: core
messageTimestamp.description: The message timestamp (millis since epoc) that this message originates from. Some systems like JMS, Kafka, AWS have a timestamp on the event/message that Camel received. This method returns the timestamp if a timestamp exists. The message timestamp and exchange created are different. An exchange always has a created timestamp which is the local timestamp when Camel created the exchange. The message timestamp is only available in some Camel components when the consumer is able to extract the timestamp from the source event. If the message has no timestamp, then 0 is returned.
examples: ${messageTimestamp} -> 1705312200000 // millis since epoch, or 0 if not available

min(val...).label: number
min(val...).description: Returns the minimum number from all the values (integral numbers only).

params.name: val
params.description: Values to compare. When omitted uses the message body (list or array)

newEmpty(type).label: collection
newEmpty(type).description: Creates a new empty object (decided by type). Use `string` to create an empty String. Use `list` to create an empty `java.util.ArrayList`. Use `map` to create an empty `java.util.LinkedHashMap`. Use `set` to create an empty `java.util.LinkedHashSet`.

params.name: type
params.description: The type to create: string, list, map, or set

normalizeWhitespace(exp).label: string
normalizeWhitespace(exp).description: Normalizes the whitespace in the message body (or expression) by cleaning up excess whitespaces.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

not.label: condition
not.description: Evaluates the predicate and returns the opposite.

originalBody.label: core
originalBody.description: The original incoming body (only available if allowUseOriginalMessage=true).

pad(exp,width,separator).label: string
pad(exp,width,separator).description: Pads the expression with extra padding if necessary, according the the total width. The separator is by default a space. If the width is negative then padding to the right, otherwise to the left.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression to pad

params.name: width
params.description: The target width. Negative for right-padding

params.name: separator
params.description: The padding character (default is space)

examples: ${pad('Hi',10)} ->         Hi // left-padded with spaces to width 10

pretty(exp).label: json,xml
pretty(exp).description: Converts the expression to a String, and attempts to pretty print if JSon or XML, otherwise the expression is returned as the String value.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

prettyBody.label: json,xml
prettyBody.description: Converts the body to a String, and attempts to pretty print if JSon or XML; otherwise the body is returned as the String value.

sort(exp,reverse).label: collection
sort(exp,reverse).description: Sorts the message body or expression in natural order

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: reverse
params.description: Whether to sort in reverse order

toPrettyJson(exp).label: json
toPrettyJson(exp).description: Converts the expression to JSon String representation.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

toPrettyJsonBody.label: json
toPrettyJsonBody.description: Converts the body to JSon String representation.

toJson(exp).label: json
toJson(exp).description: Converts the expression to JSon String representation.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

toJsonBody.label: json
toJsonBody.description: Converts the body to JSon String representation.

properties:key:default.label: core
properties:key:default.description: Lookup a property placeholder with the given key. If the key does not exist nor has a value, then an optional default value can be specified.

params.name: key
params.description: The property key to look up

params.name: default
params.description: The default value if the key is not found

examples: ${properties:myApp.port:9090} -> 9090 // uses default when key not found

propertiesExist:key.label: core
propertiesExist:key.description: Checks whether a property placeholder with the given key exists or not. The result can be negated by prefixing the key with !

params.name: key
params.description: The property key to check. Prefix with ! to negate

quote(exp).label: string
quote(exp).description: Returns the message body (or expression) as a double quoted string

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

random(min,max).label: number
random(min,max).description: Returns a random number between min and max (exclusive)

params.name: min
params.description: The minimum value (inclusive)

params.name: max
params.description: The maximum value (exclusive)

range(min,max).label: number
range(min,max).description: Returns a list of increasing integers between the given interval (exclusive)

params.name: min
params.description: The start value (inclusive)

params.name: max
params.description: The end value (exclusive)

ref:name.label: core
ref:name.description: To look up a bean from the Registry with the given name.

params.name: name
params.description: The bean name in the registry

replace(from,to,exp).label: string
replace(from,to,exp).description: Replace all the string values in the message body/expression. To make it easier to replace single and double quotes, then you can use XML escaped values `\"` as double quote, `\'` as single quote, and `\∅` as empty value.

params.name: from
params.description: The text to search for

params.name: to
params.description: The replacement text

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

reverse(val...).label: collection
reverse(val...).description: Returns a list of all the values, but in reverse order

params.name: val
params.description: Values to reverse. When omitted uses the message body

routeGroup.label: core
routeGroup.description: The route group of the current route the Exchange is being routed. Not all routes have a group assigned, so this may be null.

routeId.label: core
routeId.description: The route id of the current route the Exchange is being routed

safeQuote(exp).label: string
safeQuote(exp).description: Returns the message body (or expression) safely quoted if needed

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

setVariable(key,exp).label: attachment
setVariable(key,exp).description: Sets an attachment with payload from the message body/expression.

setHeader(name,type,exp).label: core
setHeader(name,type,exp).description: Sets a message header with the given expression (optional converting to the given type)

params.name: name
params.description: The header name to set

params.name: type
params.description: The type to convert the value to

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression value to set

examples: ${setHeader(myKey,${body})} -> sets header myKey to body value

setVariable(name,type,exp).label: core
setVariable(name,type,exp).description: Sets a variable with the given expression (optional converting to the given type)

params.name: name
params.description: The variable name to set

params.name: type
params.description: The type to convert the value to

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression value to set

examples: ${setVariable(myVar,${body})} -> sets variable myVar to body value

shuffle(val...).label: collection
shuffle(val...).description: Returns a list of all the values shuffled in random order

params.name: val
params.description: Values to shuffle. When omitted uses the message body

simpleJsonpath(input,exp).label: json
simpleJsonpath(input,exp).description: When working with JSon data, then this allows using the Simple JSonPath language, for example, to extract data from the message body (in JSon format). For input (optional), you can choose `header:key`, `exchangeProperty:key` or `variable:key` to use as input for the JSon payload instead of the message body.

params.name: input
params.description: The input source (header:key, exchangeProperty:key, variable:key). When omitted uses the message body

params.name: exp
params.description: The Simple JSonPath expression

examples: ${simpleJsonpath('$.name')} -> John // extracts name from JSON body

size(exp).label: collection
size(exp).description: Returns the number of elements in collection or array based payloads. If the value is null then 0 is returned, otherwise 1.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

examples: ${size(${body})} -> 3 // when body is a list of 3 elements

skip(num).displayName: Skip First Items from the Message Body
skip(num).label: collection
skip(num).description: The skip function iterates the message body and skips the first number of items. This can be used with the Splitter EIP to split a message body and skip the first N number of items.

params.name: num
params.description: The number of items to skip from the beginning

split(exp,separator).label: collection
split(exp,separator).description: Splits the message body/expression as a String value using the separator into a String array

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: separator
params.description: The separator to split on

stepId.label: core
stepId.description: Returns the id of the current step the Exchange is being routed.

substring(head,tail).label: string
substring(head,tail).description: Returns a substring of the message body/expression. If only one positive number, then the returned string is clipped from the beginning. If only one negative number, then the returned string is clipped from the beginning. Otherwise the returned string is clipped between the head and tail positions.

params.name: head
params.description: The start position (inclusive). Negative counts from end

params.name: tail
params.description: The end position (exclusive)

substringAfter(exp,before).label: string
substringAfter(exp,before).description: Returns a substring of the message body/expression that comes after. Returns null if nothing comes after.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: before
params.description: The delimiter to search for

substringBefore(exp,before).label: string
substringBefore(exp,before).description: Returns a substring of the message body/expression that comes before. Returns null if nothing comes before.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: before
params.description: The delimiter to search for

substringBetween(exp,after,before).label: string
substringBetween(exp,after,before).description: Returns a substring of the message body/expression that are between after and before. Returns null if nothing comes between.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: after
params.description: The start delimiter

params.name: before
params.description: The end delimiter

sum(val...).label: number
sum(val...).description: Sums together all the values as integral numbers. This function can also be used to subtract by using negative numbers.

params.name: val
params.description: Values to sum. When omitted uses the message body (list or array)

sys.name.label: other
sys.name.description: The JVM system property with the given name

params.name: name
params.description: The system property name

threadId.label: other
threadId.description: Returns the id of the current thread. Can be used for logging.

threadName.label: other
threadName.description: Returns the name of the current thread. Can be used for logging.

throwException(type,msg).label: core
throwException(type,msg).description: Deliberately throws an error. Uses IllegalArgumentException by default if no type is specified (use fully qualified classname).

params.name: type
params.description: The fully qualified exception classname

params.name: msg
params.description: The error message

trim(exp).label: string
trim(exp).description: The trim function trims the message body (or expression) by removing all leading and trailing white spaces.

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

type:name.field.label: core
type:name.field.description: To refer to a type or field by its classname. To refer to a field, you can append .FIELD_NAME. For example, you can refer to the constant field from Exchange as: `org.apache.camel.Exchange.FILE_NAME`

params.name: name
params.description: The fully qualified classname

params.name: field
params.description: The field name on the class

kindOfType(exp).label: core
kindOfType(exp).description: What kind of type is the value (null,number,string,boolean,array,object)

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

unquote(exp).label: string
unquote(exp).description: Returns the message body (or expression) with any leading/ending quotes removed

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

params.name: exp
params.description: The expression. When omitted uses the message body

uuid(type).label: other
uuid(type).description: Returns a UUID using the Camel `UuidGenerator`. You can choose between `default`, `classic`, `short` and `simple` as the type. If no type is given, the default is used. It is also possible to use a custom `UuidGenerator` and bind the bean to the Registry with an id. For example `${uuid(myGenerator)}` where the ID is _myGenerator_.

params.name: type
params.description: The UUID type: default, classic, short, simple, or a custom generator bean name

val(exp).label: core
val(exp).description: Returns the expression as a constant value

params.name: exp
params.description: The constant value expression

variable.name.label: core
variable.name.description: The variable with the given name

params.name: name
params.description: The variable name

variableAs(key,type).label: core
variableAs(key,type).description: Converts the variable to the given type (classname).

params.name: key
params.description: The variable name

params.name: type
params.description: The target type classname

variables.label: core
variables.description: Returns all the variables from the current Exchange in a Map

xpath(input,exp).label: xml
xpath(input,exp).description: When working with XML data, then this allows using the XPath language, for example, to extract data from the message body (in XML format). This requires having camel-xpath JAR on the classpath. For input (optional), you can choose `header:key`, `exchangeProperty:key` or `variable:key` to use as input for the XML payload instead of the message body.

params.name: input
params.description: The input source (header:key, exchangeProperty:key, variable:key). When omitted uses the message body

params.name: exp
params.description: The XPath expression

==.label: binary
==.description: Tests equality between left and right operand values. Camel will coerce the right operand type to match the left.

=~.label: binary
=~.description: Tests equality between left and right operand values, ignoring case for string comparison.

>.label: binary
>.description: Tests whether the left operand is greater than the right operand.

>=.label: binary
>=.description: Tests whether the left operand is greater than or equal to the right operand.

<.label: binary
<.description: Tests whether the left operand is less than the right operand.

<=.label: binary
<=.description: Tests whether the left operand is less than or equal to the right operand.

!=.label: binary
!=.description: Tests inequality between left and right operand values.

!=~.label: binary
!=~.description: Tests inequality between left and right operand values, ignoring case for string comparison.

contains.label: binary
contains.description: Tests whether the left operand string contains the right operand string.

!contains.label: binary
!contains.description: Tests whether the left operand string does not contain the right operand string.

~~.label: binary
~~.description: Tests whether the left operand string contains the right operand string, ignoring case.

!~~.label: binary
!~~.description: Tests whether the left operand string does not contain the right operand string, ignoring case.

regex.label: binary
regex.description: Tests whether the left operand matches the right operand as a regular expression.

!regex.label: binary
!regex.description: Tests whether the left operand does not match the right operand as a regular expression.

in.label: binary
in.description: Tests whether the left operand is in a set of comma-separated values.

!in.label: binary
!in.description: Tests whether the left operand is not in a set of comma-separated values.

is.label: binary
is.description: Tests whether the left operand is an instance of the right operand type (Java classname or short name).

!is.label: binary
!is.description: Tests whether the left operand is not an instance of the right operand type.

range.label: binary
range.description: Tests whether the left operand is within the numeric range specified by 'from..to'.

!range.label: binary
!range.description: Tests whether the left operand is not within the numeric range specified by 'from..to'.

startsWith.label: binary
startsWith.description: Tests whether the left operand string starts with the right operand string.

!startsWith.label: binary
!startsWith.description: Tests whether the left operand string does not start with the right operand string.

endsWith.label: binary
endsWith.description: Tests whether the left operand string ends with the right operand string.

!endsWith.label: binary
!endsWith.description: Tests whether the left operand string does not end with the right operand string.

++.label: unary
++.description: Increments the numeric value by one. Must immediately follow a function closing brace.

--.label: unary
--.description: Decrements the numeric value by one. Must immediately follow a function closing brace.

&&.label: logical
&&.description: Logical AND. Both left and right predicates must evaluate to true.

||.label: logical
||.description: Logical OR. At least one of the left or right predicates must evaluate to true.

? :.label: ternary
? :.description: Ternary conditional operator. Evaluates the predicate and returns trueValue if true, falseValue if false. Requires spaces around both ? and : tokens.

~>.label: chain
~>.description: Pipes the result of the left expression as input body to the right expression. Use $param in the right expression to reference the piped value explicitly.

?~>.label: chain
?~>.description: Null-safe chain operator. Same as ~> but stops chaining and returns null if the left expression evaluates to null.

?:.label: other
?:.description: Elvis operator (null-coalescing). Returns the left operand if it is not null/empty, otherwise returns the right operand as a fallback value.
